text.value: Please enter the QR code / Barcode of this participant
regularly = at least once a month
How many years has the child lived in this village/neighbourhood?
Scan the QR/barcode using the device
Select the name of the ID school / Village / Neighborhood

text.id: /data/p9999/p_residence/In a village more than 10 km away:label

How many people live in your household
Have you ever been treated with Albendazole or Mebendazole? (show tablet, or photo of tablet)
Why do you wash your hands
Do you regularly bathe, swim or play in nearby rivers, streams, ponds or lakes?
How many 20 litre container of water did you collect yesterday?
The repeated code must be the same as previouly entered
Are there children under 3 years old in your household?
Have you received any deworming drugs in the last 6 months?
Please re-enter the QR code / Barcode
How long does it take you to go to your main water source, get water and come back
If more than 1 facility mentioned, select multiple
L'année ne doit pas déppasé l'age de l'enfant
Select the District / LGA / Implementation Unit
Who usually goes to this source to fetch the water for your household? Probe: Is the person under age 15 years? What gender?
Other other way ti dispose stool
Vous venez d'entrer une valeur qui entraînera la fin de l'enquête
Participants must be aged between 5 and 14 years old for the supplementary survey, and between 10 and 14 years old for the PRACTICAL evaluation survey.
Please note: Additional survey: ages 5–14 / Practical assessment: ages 10–14

Important Note for Recorders: To avoid duplicate codes, Use the "Save as draft" option if you haven't finished registering all participants of this Community/Village (survey site). If you need to resume, reopen your draft from the "Saved Forms" list to continue.
Which of the following activities do you carry out in bodies of water (pond, stream, river, temporary pool of water)?
Select the name of the school / Village / Neighborhood
The order number of this participant is :
Do you share this facility with Other households?
Do you usually wash your hands with soap/ashes?
Hint: regularly = at least once a month
Enter the child's age in years (Supplementary: 5-14 years old / Practical: 10-14 years old)
Do you usually wash your hands
Do you regularly take to water to catch fish with nets or lines?
What is the main source of water used by your your household for Other purposes such as cooking and handwashing?

Important Note for Recorders: With Auto-Generated ID code option, only one phone/tablet must be used to record all participants at this Community/Village (survey site). Using multiple devices may cause duplicate ID codes, compromising data integrity.
Did parent or guardian or head teacher consent?
The last time (name of the youngest child) passed stools, what was done to dispose off the stool?
What kind of toilet facility do members of your household usually use?
If yes how many Households use this facility?
This code has already been used!
Surface water (River, Dam, Pond, Stream, Canal, Irrigation channels)
If more than 1 source mentioned, select multiple
Surface water (River, Dam, Lake, Pond, Stream, Canal, Irrigation channels)
Public toilet (in market or clini or mosque etc)
If more than 1 facility mentioned, select multiple
Please scan the QR code / Barcode of this participant
What Is the main Source of Drinking water?
Will you scann the barcode/Qrcode, use the auto-generated code by the form or enter the code manualy ?
When do you wash your hands with soap
Participant's unique code: Please copy it exactly as it appears, including on diagnostic tests.
Veuillez remercier l'enfant pour sa participation

The National Programme for the Control of NTDs, in collaboration with its partners, plans to conduct operational research in health districts, including yours.
The overall objective of this study is to assess the impact of mass treatment for SCH and STH by determining prevalence rates in the districts with a view to making appropriate treatment decisions for all health posts in the targeted districts.
In each district, several schools/villages will be selected at random.
Participation is voluntary.
Bilharzia and intestinal worms are diseases that commonly coexist in school-age children living in developing countries. The two diseases combine to cause several problems for children. These problems include low blood count (anaemia), poor physical and mental development, and can sometimes lead to death. Effective medicines such as Praziquantel, Mebendazole or Albendazole are available. These medicines are distributed annually in endemic areas to reduce morbidity and mortality associated with these diseases.
This is why we intend to conduct this study to assess the impact of these treatments on bilharzia and geohelminthiasis.
All information that you or your child provide to us as part of this study, as well as your child's test results, will remain confidential. This information will not be shared with anyone outside the study team.
Do you treat your water in any way to make it safer to drink?
Please enter the order number of this participant
Have you ever been treated with Praziquantel? (show tablet, or photo of tablet)

item.value: In a village more than 10 km away
